COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-04-2017 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day, having been assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing.
Judge: Angela Bradstreet, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Daniel J. Cravens, Esq. appeared with defendant/cross-complainant Jennifer E. Crane, who was placed under oath and testified.
Plaintiff/cross-defendant Kazim Acar failed to appear for this morning's proceedings.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was served on January 26,2017, setting trial for today, December 4, 2017.
Plaintiff's complaint was dismissed on July 19, 2017 for failure to file an undertaking.

The Court admitted into evidence the following exhibits submitted by cross-complainant:

Exhibit 6 - Copies of text message exchanges betweeen Jennifer Crane and Kazim Acar;
Exhibit 8 - Copy of emails from Kazim Acar to Jennifer Crane;
Exhibit 9 - Copy of the April 27, 2015 letter from Jennifer Crane to Kazim Acar;
Exhibit 10 - Copy of email exchange between Kazim Acar and Jennifer Crane;
Exhibit 20 - Billing Statement from the San Francisco Fire Department to Cross-Complainant Jennifer Crane dated August 11, 2015.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court enters judgment in favor of cross-complainant Jennifer E. Crane and against cross-defendant Kazim Acar, as follows:

Security Deposit (Paid to Acar) - $3,900.00.
Sixty-five Days Rent @ 65.00 per day - $390.00.
Costs Incurred for Hotel Stay (5 nights) - $390.00.
Optomotrist Expenses - $188.00.
Ambulance Fee - $1,974.00.
Emergency Room Visit - $1,300.00.

Total - $8,142.00.

Cross-complainant has proved-up her prima facie case on causes of action numbers 1, 2, 3, 4, 6 & 7. The Court DENIES cross-complainant's FEHA cause of action as she (Ms. Crane) failed to exhaust all her administrative remedies. The Court further finds that cross-defendant Kazim Acar acted in bad faith, and that cross-complainant shall recover her attorney fees through noticed motion set in Department 514. Counsel stipulates to the return of the exhibits.

Damages are hereby TREBLED in accordance with San Francisco Rent Ordinance Section 37.10B(5).

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the Proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.
